economic_notes: TFN and TAA receive revenues from mining activities on N'Daki Menan under Impact Benefits Agreements. During the year, amounts earned under these agreements totaled $1,044,652. Assets from these agreements ($2.776 million) are not consolidated. TFN entered a 10-year New Fiscal Relationship agreement with ISC commencing April 1, 2020. OFNLP provides programs in Education, Health, Economic, Cultural and Community Development. DMLRC operates fuelwood business, office and property rentals and forestry operations.
notes: Consolidates Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership (OFNLP) and Daki Menan Lands and Resources Corporation (DMLRC). Impact Benefits Agreement assets ($2.776M) and activities are excluded from consolidation as not controlled by TFN. Long-term debt includes a $1,000,582 contingent loan payable to INAC contingent on land claim settlement. No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council or senior staff included in the financial statements. Health and social expenses reported as combined segment; social expenses not separately broken out. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC, Health Canada, Government of Canada, CMHC, Province of Ontario, and Other Organizations transfers. TFN obtained First Nations Financial Management Board certification March 27, 2020.
